concept: Wander / Roam / Move Without Fixed Destination
description: To move about without a fixed route or destination—exploring, meandering, following curiosity rather than plan. 'Whenola' names the freedom of undirected movement, whether physical rambling through landscape or mental drifting through ideas. The wanderer is not lost but open.
sound_symbolism: The word drifts like wandering itself. 'Whe' /ʍe/ begins with voiceless breath, movement without force or urgency. Then 'no' suggests no fixed destination, direction undefined. Finally 'la' flows with liquid ease, path following its own logic.
grammatical_notes: Functions as intransitive verb: 'mi whenola' (I wander). Can take locative: 'mi mua sipho whenola' (I wander in/through the garden). Distinct from journey (purposeful travel); whenola implies openness to wherever paths lead.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Walking meditation can become wandering meditation—attention present, destination released, each step complete in itself.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The wandering line defines art nouveau—curves that follow their own organic logic rather than imposed geometry.
